channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent, tapped to run CFPB, orders staff to halt work
item.description: Staff at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau were instructed by email to cease much of their work. Bessent, a wealthy Wall Street investor, replaces Rohit Chopra, who was fired on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Federal agencies scrub climate change from websites amid Trump rebranding
item.description: Federal agencies are beginning to remove references to climate change from their websites, as Trump issued a rebranding of departments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk-led DOGE effectively shuts down USAID
item.description: Democratic lawmakers say shutting down the U.S. Agency for International Development is illegal, vowing legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration's data deletions set off 'a mad scramble,' researcher says
item.description: Researchers across the country are in “a mad scramble” to sort out what public data the Trump administration has deleted from government websites.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe And Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Even those who have not worked directly in DEI roles but went to the training have been put on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Block on Trump administration federal funding freeze will likely be extended, Washington judge says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge in the nation's capital will likely keep a temporary block on a Trump administration plan for a freeze on federal funding after lawyers for nonprofit groups said they're still struggling to get promised grants and loans. U.S. District Judge Loren L.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk is a 'special government employee,' the White House confirms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is working for President Donald Trump as a “special government employee," according to a White House official, solidifying his controversial role in the administration but sidestepping some disclosure rules that are typical of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems claim Trump tariff could 'drive up' costs despite deflecting blame from Biden's inflation
item.description: Democratic lawmakers criticizing President Donald Trump's tariff on Canada, Mexico and China after supporting several tax hikes under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of Education Department workers are put on leave over Trump's anti-DEI order
item.description: A labor union for federal workers says dozens of employees at the U.S. Education Department have been put on paid administrative leave in response to President Donald Trump’s order banning diversity, equity and inclusion programs in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump announces pause on Mexico tariffs after striking deal
item.description: Trump revealed he negotiated assistance from Mexico to strengthen the United States' southern border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth to visit Fort Bliss, southern border
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Fort Bliss and the southern border on Monday, Feb. 3, Fort Bliss announced. Hegseth will visit Joint Task Force North, which is headquartered at Fort Bliss. This organization is part of U.S. Northern Command.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, Canada after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is holding off on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada for 30 days after the two U.S. neighbors agreed to boost border security efforts.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Virginia Attorney General asks Costco to end DEI policies
item.description: Virginia Attorney General Jason Miyares is urging the CEO of Costco to end "unlawful" diversity, equity and inclusion policies at the company.
item.dc:creator: Marysa Tuttle, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers clash over immigration policies, in-state tuition for Dreamers
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — As Gov. Ron DeSantis and legislative leaders in Tallahassee clash on immigration proposals, DeSantis said he's planning to veto the recently passed TRUMP Act. That move could put lawmakers back to square one.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican border cities can expect higher unemployment, bankruptcies with Trump tariffs, Baja business leaders say
item.description: According to Vega Álvarez, consumers on both sides of the border will be the ones who will pay for the tariffs as "things will have to be overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats demand Patel information in special counsel's classified documents report
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee are demanding to see the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with the Mar-a-Lago classified documents investigation, asserting it has relevant information about Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Supreme Court declines to dismiss 'fake electors' appeal
item.description: The Nevada Supreme Court has declined to dismiss an appeal involving the six Republicans charged after falsely claiming President Donald Trump won the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Nevada voters united on top issues while Harris backers couldn't, NVPOP poll shows
item.description: A new poll that examines the driving forces behind Nevada voters' decisions in the 2024 election shows that President Donald Trump's support came from people who were focused on two primary issues: the economy and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
